title: $formdbmod (deprecated)
text: $formdbmod (deprecated) Return the modification status of database fields in the current Form. Note: The $formdbmod function is deprecated. It has been superseded by $instancedbmod , which is applicable to all component types. For more information, see $instancedbmod . Return = $formdbmod Return Values Table 1. Values Returned in $formdbmod Value Meaning 0 Field has not been modified; this can be because: No database field has been modified. No database occurrences have been added or removed. No entities are painted on the form. 1 Field has been modified; this can be due to: A database field in a database entity has been modified. An database occurrence has been added to or removed from the component. Use Use in Form components. Description Use $formdbmod to test whether any database field has been modified. Non-database fields ( Is external =false ) do not affect $formdbmod . The value of $formdbmod is actually the inclusive logical OR of the values of the $fieldmod function for all database fields in the form. Statements that affect the value of $formdbmod are the same as those that affect $instancedbmod , and the return values are determined in the same way. Related Links $instancedbmod Return the modification status of database fields in the current component instance. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
